title: A Quiet Dinner After Midnight
content: After finishing another exhausting shift at the restaurant, Noah walked through nearly empty streets illuminated by pale streetlights and distant traffic signals. It was already past midnight by the time he entered the small diner near his apartment. Only a few tired customers sat scattered around the room in silence. Noah ordered soup and coffee before settling into a booth near the window. Outside, the city still moved restlessly despite the late hour. As he ate slowly, he noticed how peaceful those quiet moments felt compared to the noise and pressure of the kitchen. Sometimes, the loneliness of late nights carried a strange comfort that daytime never seemed able to offer.
